excerpts.text: You are reading this book for two reasons. First, you are a programmer. Second, you want to be a better programmer. Good. We need better programmers.

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 0 Chapter 1
table_of_contents.title: Evolution, the Themes of Biology, and Scientific Inquiry; Inquiring About Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 1.1
table_of_contents.title: The study of life reveals common themes

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 1.2
table_of_contents.title: The Core Theme: Evolution accounts for the unity and diversity of life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 1.3
table_of_contents.title: In studying nature, scientists make observations and form and test hypotheses

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 1.4
table_of_contents.title: Science benefits from a cooperative approach and diverse viewpoints

table_of_contents.title: The Chemical Context of Life; A Chemical Connection to Biology

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 2.1
table_of_contents.title: Matter consists of chemical elements in pure form and in combinations called compounds

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 2.2
table_of_contents.title: An element’s properties depend on the structure of its atoms

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 2.3
table_of_contents.title: The formation and function of molecules depend on chemical bonding between atoms

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 2.4
table_of_contents.title: Chemical reactions make and break chemical bonds

table_of_contents.title: Water and Life; The Molecule That Supports All of Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 3.1
table_of_contents.title: Polar covalent bonds in water molecules result in hydrogen bonding

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 3.2
table_of_contents.title: Four emergent properties of water contribute to Earth’s suitability for life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 3.3
table_of_contents.title: Acidic and basic conditions affect living organisms

table_of_contents.title: Carbon and the Molecular Diversity of Life; Carbon: The Backbone of Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 4.1
table_of_contents.title: Organic chemistry is the study of carbon compounds

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 4.2
table_of_contents.title: Carbon atoms can form diverse molecules by bonding to four other atoms

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 4.3
table_of_contents.title: A few chemical groups are key to molecular function

table_of_contents.title: The Structure and Function of Large Biological Molecules ; The Molecules of Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.1
table_of_contents.title: Macromolecules are polymers, built from monomers

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.2
table_of_contents.title: Carbohydrates serve as fuel and building material

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.3
table_of_contents.title: Lipids are a diverse group of hydrophobic molecules

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.4
table_of_contents.title: Proteins include a diversity of structures, resulting in a wide range of functions

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.5
table_of_contents.title: Nucleic acids store, transmit, and help express hereditary information

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 5.6
table_of_contents.title: Genomics and proteomics have transformed biological inquiry and applications

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 2 THE CELL Chapter 6
table_of_contents.title: A Tour of the Cell; The Fundamental Units of Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.1
table_of_contents.title: Biologists use microscopes and biochemistry to study cells

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.2
table_of_contents.title: Eukaryotic cells have internal membranes that compartmentalize their functions

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.3
table_of_contents.title: The eukaryotic cell’s genetic instructions are housed in the nucleus and carried out by the ribosomes

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.4
table_of_contents.title: The endomembrane system regulates protein traffic and performs metabolic functions

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.5
table_of_contents.title: Mitochondria and chloroplasts change energy from one form to another

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.6
table_of_contents.title: The cytoskeleton is a network of fibers that organizes structures and activities in the cell

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.7
table_of_contents.title: Extracellular components and connections between cells help coordinate cellular activities

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 6.8
table_of_contents.title: A cell is greater than the sum of its parts

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 2 THE CELL Chapter 7
table_of_contents.title: Membrane Structure and Function; Life at the Edge

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 7.1
table_of_contents.title: Cellular membranes are fluid mosaics of lipids and proteins

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 7.2
table_of_contents.title: Membrane structure results in selective permeability

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 7.3
table_of_contents.title: Passive transport is diffusion of a substance across a membrane with no energy investment

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 7.4
table_of_contents.title: Active transport uses energy to move solutes against their gradients

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 7.5
table_of_contents.title: Bulk transport across the plasma membrane occurs by exocytosis and endocytosis

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 2 THE CELL Chapter 8
table_of_contents.title: An Introduction to Metabolism; The Energy of Life

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 8.1
table_of_contents.title: An organism’s metabolism transforms matter and energy, subject to the laws of thermodynamics

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 8.2
table_of_contents.title: The free-energy change of a reaction tells us whether or not the reaction occurs spontaneously

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 8.3
table_of_contents.title: ATP powers cellular work by coupling exergonic reactions to endergonic reactions

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 8.4
table_of_contents.title: Enzymes speed up metabolic reactions by lowering energy barriers

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 8.5
table_of_contents.title: Regulation of enzyme activity helps control metabolism

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 2 THE CELL Chapter 9
table_of_contents.title: Cellular Respiration and Fermentation; Life Is Work

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.1
table_of_contents.title: Catabolic pathways yield energy by oxidizing organic fuels

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.2
table_of_contents.title: Glycolysis harvests chemical energy by oxidizing glucose to pyruvate

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.3
table_of_contents.title: After pyruvate is oxidized, the citric acid cycle completes the energy-yielding oxidation of organic molecules

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.4
table_of_contents.title: During oxidative phosphorylation, chemiosmosis couples electron transport to ATP synthesis

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.5
table_of_contents.title: Fermentation and anaerobic respiration enable cells to produce ATP without the use of oxygen

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 9.6
table_of_contents.title: Glycolysis and the citric acid cycle connect to many other metabolic pathways

table_of_contents.label: UNIT 2 THE CELL Chapter 10
table_of_contents.title: Photosynthesis; The Process That Feeds the Biosphere

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 10.1
table_of_contents.title: Photosynthesis converts light energy to the chemical energy of food

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 10.2
table_of_contents.title: The light reactions convert solar energy to the chemical energy of ATP and NADPH

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 10.3
table_of_contents.title: The Calvin cycle uses the chemical energy of ATP and NADPH to reduce CO2 to sugar

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 10.4
table_of_contents.title: Alternative mechanisms of carbon fixation have evolved in hot, arid climates

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 11.1
table_of_contents.title: External signals are converted to responses within the cell

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 11.2
table_of_contents.title: Reception: A signaling molecule binds to a receptor protein, causing it to change shape

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 11.3
table_of_contents.title: Transduction: Cascades of molecular interactions relay signals from receptors to target molecules in the cell

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 11.4
table_of_contents.title: Response: Cell signaling leads to regulation of transcription or cytoplasmic activities

table_of_contents.title: The Cell Cycle; The Key Roles of Cell Division

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 12.1
table_of_contents.title: Most cell division results in genetically identical daughter cells

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 12.2
table_of_contents.title: The mitotic phase alternates with interphase in the cell cycle

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 12.3
table_of_contents.title: The eukaryotic cell cycle is regulated by a molecular control system

table_of_contents.title: Offspring acquire genes from parents by inheriting chromosomes

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 13.2
table_of_contents.title: Fertilization and meiosis alternate in sexual life cycles

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 13.3
table_of_contents.title: Meiosis reduces the number of chromosome sets from diploid to haploid

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 13.4
table_of_contents.title: Genetic variation produced in sexual life cycles contributes to evolution

table_of_contents.title: Mendel used the scientific approach to identify two laws of inheritance

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 14.3
table_of_contents.title: Inheritance patterns are often more complex than predicted by simple Mendelian genetics

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 14.4
table_of_contents.title: Many human traits follow Mendelian patterns of inheritance

table_of_contents.title: Many proteins work together in DNA replication and repair

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 16.3
table_of_contents.title: A chromosome consists of a DNA molecule packed together with proteins

table_of_contents.title: Transcription is the DNA-directed synthesis of RNA: a closer look

table_of_contents.title: Mutations of one or a few nucleotides can affect protein structure and function

table_of_contents.title: Eukaryotic gene expression is regulated at many stages

table_of_contents.title: A program of differential gene expression leads to the different cell types in a multicellular organism

table_of_contents.title: Cancer results from genetic changes that affect cell cycle control

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 19.1

table_of_contents.title: Viruses replicate only in host cells

table_of_contents.title: Natural selection, genetic drift, and gene flow can alter allele frequencies in a population

table_of_contents.label: CONCEPT 33.1
